CREATE TABLE sales (id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), sale_date DATE);
CREATE TABLE employees (id INT, name VARCHAR(50), sales_id INT);
SELECT first_value(amount) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date) FROM sales;
SELECT e.name, first_value(s.amount) OVER (PARTITION BY e.id ORDER BY s.sale_date) AS first_sale_amount FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.sales_id = s.id;
WITH ranked_sales AS (SELECT amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY sale_date) AS rnk FROM sales) SELECT first_value(amount) OVER (ORDER BY rnk) FROM ranked_sales;
SELECT e.name, first_value(s.amount) OVER (PARTITION BY e.id ORDER BY s.sale_date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.sales_id = s.id;
SELECT e.name, first_value(s.amount) OVER (PARTITION BY e.id ORDER BY sale_date ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS first_sale_current FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.sales_id = s.id;
CREATE TABLE temp_sales (id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2));
INSERT INTO temp_sales SELECT * FROM sales WHERE amount > 100;
SELECT first_value(amount) OVER (ORDER BY id) FROM temp_sales;
SELECT e.name, first_value(s.amount) OVER (PARTITION BY e.name ORDER BY s.sale_date) FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.sales_id = s.id;
SELECT first_value(amount) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date DESC) FROM sales;
SELECT e.name, first_value(s.amount) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date ASC RANGE BETWEEN INTERVAL '1' DAY PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS first_sale_in_range FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.sales_id = s.id;
